_comment: Founder picked Option A after AWS rejected the original lock 'hot 14d → IA 30d'
(STANDARD_IA has a 30-day storage-class minimum). Adjusted schedule preserves
founder spirit (IA middle tier + 18 months in Glacier) by bumping hot to 30d:
Day 30-60: STANDARD_IA (30 days in IA, exactly satisfies AWS minimum)
Day 60-607: GLACIER Flexible (547 days = 18 months, well past Glacier's 90-day minimum)
Total lifetime: 607 days (~20 months from creation to expiration).
Noncurrent versions (created when files are overwritten — e.g. _verification-log.jsonl)
follow the same tiering but age out separately so the append-only history is preserved
for the full retention window before garbage collection.
AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload after 7 days catches stuck uploads that won't otherwise
be visible — common cause of mystery bills on snapshot buckets.
